text: I want to talk with people from other countries.
ko: 다른 나라 사람들과 이야기하고 싶어요.
examples: I want to talk with people from other countries.
I'd love to chat with people from all over the world.
paraphrases: I'd love to chat with people from all over the world.
My goal is to communicate easily with people from different countries.
I really want to be able to hold a conversation with foreigners.
I aspire to communicate fluently with people from various cultures.
distractors: I want to talk people from other countries.
I want talk with people from other countries.
I want to talking with people from other countries.

text: The essence of studying a language is talking with people.
ko: 언어를 공부하는 것의 본질은 사람들과 대화하는 것이다.
examples: The essence of studying a language is talking with people.
The whole point of learning English is to communicate.
paraphrases: The whole point of learning a language is talking with people.
At its core, language learning is about communication.
The heart of studying a language is real conversation.
distractors: The essence of study a language is talking with people.
The essence of studying a language is talk with people.
The essence of studying a language is to talking with people.

text: I can spend 30 minutes a day studying English.
ko: 하루에 30분 영어 공부에 쓸 수 있어요.
examples: I can spend 30 minutes a day studying English.
I set aside half an hour every evening to study.
paraphrases: I can set aside 30 minutes a day for English.

distractors: I can spend 30 minutes a day to study English.

text: How do you say this in English?
ko: 이거 영어로 어떻게 말해요?
examples: How do you say this in English?
What's the English word for this?
paraphrases: What's the English word for this?
How would you put this in English?

distractors: How do you say this on English?

text: Could you say that again, please?
ko: 다시 말씀해 주시겠어요?
examples: Could you say that again, please?
Sorry, could you repeat that?
paraphrases: Sorry, could you repeat that?
Would you mind saying that one more time?
I didn't catch that — could you say it again?
distractors: Could you saying that again, please?

text: In my opinion, speaking every day is more important than perfect grammar.
ko: 제 생각엔, 매일 말하는 게 완벽한 문법보다 더 중요해요.
examples: In my opinion, speaking every day is more important than perfect grammar.
I think daily speaking beats perfect grammar.
paraphrases: I think daily practice matters more than being grammatically perfect.
From my perspective, consistent speaking beats perfect grammar.
I'd say fluency comes from practice, not perfection.
distractors: In my opinion, speak every day is more important than perfect grammar.
In my opinion, speaking every day is more important then perfect grammar.

text: I tend to forget vocabulary if I don't review it.
ko: 복습하지 않으면 단어를 잊어버리는 경향이 있어요.
examples: I tend to forget vocabulary if I don't review it.
I usually forget new words unless I practise them regularly.
paraphrases: I usually forget words if I don't practise them.
Without review, I end up forgetting new vocabulary.
If I skip review, I lose the words I've learned.
distractors: I tend to forgetting vocabulary if I don't review it.

text: What I meant was, I find it difficult, not impossible.
ko: 제 말은, 어렵다는 거지 불가능하다는 게 아니에요.
examples: What I meant was, I find it difficult, not impossible.
What I was trying to say was it's challenging, but not out of reach.
paraphrases: What I was trying to say was it's tough, not hopeless.

To put it differently, it's challenging but definitely doable.
distractors: What I meant were, I find it difficult, not impossible.

text: Let me rephrase that — I think mistakes help us grow.
ko: 다시 말하면 — 실수가 우리를 성장하게 한다고 생각해요.
examples: Let me rephrase that — I think mistakes help us grow.
Actually, let me say it differently: errors are how we learn.
paraphrases: To put it another way, errors are part of learning.
What I mean is, mistakes are valuable, not something to fear.

distractors: Let me to rephrase that — I think mistakes help us grow.

text: I used to be nervous about speaking English, but now I enjoy it.
ko: 예전엔 영어로 말하는 게 긴장됐는데, 이제는 즐거워요.
examples: I used to be nervous about speaking English, but now I enjoy it.

paraphrases: I once found speaking English scary, but I've grown to love it.

examples: Lately, I've been struggling with pronunciation more than grammar.

examples: Why don't we try watching English dramas without subtitles?

text: You might want to keep a vocabulary notebook.

text: I've been studying English for about five years, but speaking is still the hardest part.
ko: 영어를 약 5년간 공부해 왔는데, 말하기가 여전히 가장 어려운 부분이에요.
examples: I've been studying English for about five years, but speaking is still the hardest part.
I've been learning English since middle school, though I've mostly focused on reading.
paraphrases: It's been about five years since I started studying English, and speaking is still my weakest area.
I've put in around five years of English study, but I still struggle to speak naturally.
I've been at this for five years now, yet speaking fluently is still a challenge for me.
distractors: I've been studying English since about five years, but speaking is still the hardest part.

text: Have you ever had a moment when everything just clicked?
ko: 모든 게 딱 맞아 떨어지는 순간을 경험한 적 있어요?
examples: Have you ever had a moment when everything just clicked?

paraphrases: Have you ever had a moment where it all suddenly made sense?

text: If I had more free time, I would immerse myself in English every day.
ko: 시간이 더 있다면, 매일 영어에 완전히 빠져들 텐데요.
examples: If I had more free time, I would immerse myself in English every day.

paraphrases: If I weren't so busy, I'd dedicate more time to English every day.

text: What would you do if you could speak English perfectly?

text: Despite being nervous, I tried speaking English with a stranger.

text: The thing that I find hardest about English is speaking without thinking.

text: I'd love to find a conversation partner who speaks only in English.
